Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|qq=|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|qq=|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|qq=|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|qq=|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|qq=|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

[[ja:霜月神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11660782}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霜月神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|qq=|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|qq=|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|qq=|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|qq=|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|qq=|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].

[[ja:霜月神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11660782}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霜月神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|qq=|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|qq=|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|qq=|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|qq=|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|qq=|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|qq=|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|qq=|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|qq=|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - [[ja:霜月神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11660782}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霜月神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].

{{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霜月神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - == References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霜月神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11660782]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuki kagura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsuki Kagura''' (霜月神楽) refers to [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances held during the 11th month of the old lunar calendar (霜月), corresponding to [[:en:December|December]] through [[:en:January|January]] of the following year in the Gregorian calendar. It is also known as '''{{ill|Yudate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}''' because it is often performed in conjunction with the {{ill|Yudate|ja|湯立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudate|1=yudate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563775}} ritual. Additionally, as it is believed to be modeled after the kagura of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is sometimes called '''{{ill|Ise-ryū Kagura|ja|伊勢流神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-ryū Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霜月神楽|1=Shimotsuki kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660782}}'''.
== Overview ==
Kagura was traditionally considered an event to be held in the 11th month of the old calendar. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient]] and [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval Japan]], the kagura of the {{ill|Sonkara Shrine|ja|園韓神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonkara Shrine|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} at the Imperial Court was performed in November. Additionally, on the 13th day of the 11th lunar month, kagura ceremonies (known as {{ill|Yoriai Kagura|ja|寄合神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoriai Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōnō Kagura|ja|奉納神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) were held at the residence of the {{ill|Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja|一口頭大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitokuchi Tōdai-fu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the {{ill|Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshi of Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Onshi|WD=Q11488972}}.
Although the kagura of the Ise Goshi declined during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it had previously spread across Japan, blending with elements of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}, [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], and {{ill|Shushō-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shushō-e|1=Shushō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}}.
In the old calendar, November was considered a time when the gods and nature weakened. Therefore, kagura was performed to revitalize and renew the spirit in preparation for the new year. Rituals from Kumano, such as purification with sacred hot water (湯の清まり) and secluded purification practices (忌籠), were incorporated, becoming the basis for Yudate.
The ritual stage (庭, niwa) is adorned with sacred symbols such as [[:en:Gohei|Gohei]] and [[:en:Himorogi|Himorogi]]. A hot water cauldron (湯釜, yugama) is placed at the center, with canopies representing clouds and the heavens hanging above. Sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) and paper decorations ({{ill|Kirigami|ja|切紙飾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) encircle the area to welcome the gods. Throughout the night, various prayers, ritual recitations, and kagura dances performed by priests and participants are conducted around the cauldron.
While the specific methods of Shimotsuki Kagura vary by region and shrine, the general ritual sequence includes drawing sacred water from rivers or waterfalls, heating it in cauldrons, offering it to the gods, and sprinkling it on participants for purification ([[:en:Harae|Harae]] and [[:en:Misogi|Misogi]]). The ceremony begins with the reading of a {{ill|Shinmeichō|ja|神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeichō|1=Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588822}} (register of invited deities), followed by:
- Inviting the gods with maskless dances (採物舞, torimono-mai) - Offering praises (願上, negaiage) - Sending off the deities around midnight (神送り, kami-okuri) - A sacred feast (直会, naorai) - Later-night performances featuring masked characters like demons and elders (面形舞, omote-mai) - A final blessing ceremony
Special honors are given to local deities, even if they are of lower Rank, due to their deep connection with the community.
== Notable Shimotsuki Kagura Performances ==
[[File:Toyama's Shimotsuki Festival 001.JPG|thumb|Statue from the {{ill|Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Shimotsuki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A hot spring (飲泉) modeled after the Yudate ritual is visible on the right, and the water is drinkable. Photo taken at {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|ja|道の駅遠山郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Toyama-go|1=Roadside station Toyamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641523}} in 2009 ([[:en:2009|Heisei 21]]).]]
Here are some representative examples of Shimotsuki Kagura. Many other kagura traditions that have preserved parts or all of the Shimotsuki Kagura ritual can be found throughout Japan.
- {{ill|Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|保呂羽山の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Horowasan no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385698}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} in {{ill|Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県横手市大森町八沢木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori, Yokote City, Akita Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|ja|天龍村の霜月神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|1=Tenryū-mura no Shimotsuki Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443804}}: A designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県下伊那郡天龍村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja|遠山の霜月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyama no Shimotsuki Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A festival held in former [[:en:Minamishinano, Nagano|Minami-Shinano Village]] and [[:en:Kami, Nagano|Kamimura]] in {{ill|Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県飯田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida City, Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. - {{ill|Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|ja|花祭 (霜月神楽)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hana Matsuri (Shimotsuki Kagura)|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219854}}: A traditional folk performance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in the {{ill|Okumikawa|ja|奥三河|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumikawa|1=Okumikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445584}} region of [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]].
== References == * Itaya Toru, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}}* 2 ([[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 2000), ISBN 978-4-095-23002-3. * Kubota Hiromichi, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihon Minzoku Daijiten* Vol. 1 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1998), ISBN 978-4-642-01332-1. * Yamaji Kōzō, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *Nihonshi Daijiten* 3 ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Aoki Shūhei, "Shimotsuki Kagura," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}* 7 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986), ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4. == See Also == * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|ja|冬祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}}
